title: 'I started to love basketball again': How Michigan built a champion on fun and freedom
content: In the year of the freshman, Dusty May led a band of transfers and upperclassmen to a national title.

title: Michigan hits 'ultimate target,' wins NCAA title
content: Michigan won its second national title by becoming the first team since 2009 to beat UConn in the Sweet 16 or later.

title: Hurley, Karaban reflective after UConn bows out
content: In their last game together, UConn coach Dan Hurley and Huskies star Alex Karaban saw a bid for a third national title in four years come to an end at the hands of the Michigan Wolverines, who produced a 69-63 victory at Lucas Oil Stadium on Monday night.

title: Wemby leaves Spurs' victory due to bruised rib
content: The Spurs ruled out Victor Wembanyama for the second half of Monday's win against the 76ers because of a left rib contusion.

title: Contreras warns Brewers: Hit me again, you'll pay
content: Willson Contreras had a warning for the Brewers after he was hit by a pitch for the 24th time by the team Monday. "... Next time they hit me again, I'm going to take one of them out. That's a message."

title: Grizzlies tie NBA mark for 3s with 29 but still lose
content: The Grizzlies tied the NBA single-game record with 29 3-pointers on Monday but still lost 142-126 at home to the Cavaliers.

title: Lady Vols lose recruit Edwards, see roster cut to 1
content: On the same day that Tennessee guard Jaida Civil announced she's entering the transfer portal, Oliviyah Edwards, the No. 2 recruit in the SC Next 100 class of 2026, requested a release from her national letter of intent with the Lady Volunteers on Monday.

title: Cooper irked as penalty box injury sparks probe
content: The NHL is looking into the circumstances that led to Tampa Bay Lightning forward Pontus Holmberg hurting his left arm when he crashed shoulder-first into an unlatched penalty box door in Monday night's 4-2 loss to the Buffalo Sabres at KeyBank Center.

title: Sources: UNC to hire Malone as new hoops coach
content: North Carolina intends to hire former longtime NBA coach Michael Malone as the school's next basketball coach, sources told ESPN.

content: Young talent, diving catches, home runs and ... bobbleheads? Here are baseball's most exciting squads for 2026.

content: NBA insiders break down lingering questions, lottery races and what to watch in the final week of the 2025-26 NBA regular season.

content: After the recent tumult, Woods won't be in Augusta, but Masters fans still seek the ghosts of past miracle shots. There is no unwinding this tournament and Tiger.

content: Only eight players have pulled off the feat in the same season. Who are the most likely "double champs?"

content: The Bulls fired vice president of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas and general manager Marc Eversley on Monday. So ... what now? Here's what our insiders are hearing.

title: 'The best decision I ever made': How UCLA's roster converged to become NCAA champs
content: How a longtime coach and an eclectic mix of transfers finally delivered an NCAA title years in the making.

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25 rankings for the 2026-27 season
content: UCLA is the 2026 champion -- but unranked in the first top 25 for next season. Runner-up South Carolina is No. 3.

title: Hans Niemann plots a path from chess villain to vindication
content: Everybody heard the outlandish cheating accusations. Now Hans Niemann is ready to fight for his reputation, and a chess world championship.

title: Barcelona close on LaLiga title, Man City thrash Liverpool, more
content: Barcelona took a huge step toward winning LaLiga with a victory over Atlético, while Man City caused Liverpool more pain with an FA Cup rout for the ages. Gab Marcotti recaps the weekend.

title: MLS Power Rankings: LAFC are practically perfect atop the table
content: LAFC still hasn't conceded in league play in 2026, only a draw blemishes its record this season, but it's enough to top ESPN's MLS Power Rankings.

title: Five takeaways from NCAA regionals -- and what they mean for nationals
content: Chiles and Chio continued their superstar ways. Oklahoma, Florida and LSU dominated. After NCAA gymnastics regionals, the final eight teams are set.

title: How A.J. Terrell Jr. mentored his brother, Avieon, who's set to join the All-Pro in the NFL
content: Set to be a first-round cornerback in the NFL draft like his brother, Avieon Terrell has looked to A.J. as his inspiration throughout his life.

content: Here are the players who can help fill holes on your roster during the final week of the fantasy basketball season.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United, Liverpool fight for Monaco forward
content: Manchester United and Liverpool are stepping up interest in AS Monaco star Maghnes Akliouche. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Kerala elections: Win or lose, Congress could ‘transition into BJP’ in Kerala, alleges CPI(M) leader M. Swaraj
content: Swaraj cites instances of Congress MLAs joining BJP in different States and warns the electorate in Kerala that such a scenario is possible in the State too

title: 100-year-old problem of farmers resolved with issuance of pattadar passbooks
content: The farmers of these village have been cultivating the lands that they received from their ancestors for nearly 10 decades, however the records did not reflect their ownership

title: India and the second Space Age
content: India and the second Space Age offers a comprehensive survey of the challenges and opportunities ahead.

title: Assam Police at Pawan Khera's residence in New Delhi; Himanta says Congress leader ‘ran away’
content: The development comes amid a political tussle after Assam CM Sarma alleged that the Congress used "false information" sourced from a Pakistani social media group to level charges against his wife

title: SC hearing on Sabarimala women entry LIVE: 'Courts interfere when social evils are branded as religious practice'
content: The 2018 judgement that allowed women of menstruating age to enter Ayyappa temple in Sabarimala has compressed the plurality and diversity of Hindusim, argues Centre

title: Karnataka second in OMC-run EV charging network with 2,039 stations
content: Karnataka is ahead of major States such as Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Gujarat in terms of operational OMC-run charging stations. Only Uttar Pradesh, with 2,893 stations, has a larger network

title: Police convene meeting of gym owners in Hubballi following protests against ‘love jihad’
content: During protests, Hindutva outfits raised several issues pertaining to gym trainers and security measures in gymnasiums

title: Fuel mismanagement leading to ‘full-blown livelihood crisis’: Congress
content: Congress leaders described the situation as a 'failure of leadership', stating that the Centre's inaction was pushing the working class towards distress

title: ‘Corruption caused floods’: Congress MLA Kuzhalnadan claims new audio evidence ties LDF govt to 2018 Kuttanad disaster
content: Mathew Kuzhalnadan alleges LDF govt’s ‘corruption-driven wilful delay’ in opening shutters of Thottappally spillway caused devastating floods in Kerala’s rice bowl. Minister Krishnankutty denies allegations; Pinarayi says ‘desperation of impending defeat (in polls) has prompted Congress to broadcast old lies’

title: Mumbai Metro lines 2B and 9 to be launched today: All you need to know
content: Metro Line 9 marks the first metro corridor in the Thane district, while Line 2B extends metro connectivity to key Harbour Line areas, including Chembur and adjoining localities.

title: 2026 Assembly elections schedule: Dates for Assam, West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry
content: CEC Gyanesh Kumar on March 15, 2026, announced that the 2026 State Assembly elections will take place in 824 constituencies, with approximately 17.4 crore eligible voters participating in the democratic process

title: Leopard shot dead near Periyapatna, Karnataka Minister orders probe into suspected poaching
content: A post-mortem examination confirmed that three bullets were lodged in the animal’s body, establishing that the leopard was killed by gunfire

title: J&K Police bust inter-State Lashkar-e-Taiba racket; terror leader on run for 16 years among five nabbed
content: Investigators searched 19 locations, including in Jammu and Kashmir, Rajasthan and Haryana, and recovered incriminating material

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026: How Kerala has voted since 2011
content: Kerala's political landscape has traditionally been characterised by a swing in leadership between Left Democratic Front (LDF) and United Democratic Front (UDF), except in 2021 elections when LDF retained power

title: BJP will draw a blank this time too, says Pinarayi Vijayan
content: The Kerala Chief Minister doesn’t see defections from the Left helping either the BJP or the Congress, which, according to him, is the BJP’s ‘B team’; for the State to be in step with the modern era, the Left should continue to be in govt, he argues

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: past scandals and dramatis personae that rocked elections in Kerala
content: Election-time political scandals seem to have a short lifespan in Kerala. However, the ephemeral sensational value of scandals, especially sordid ones, will likely endure

title: 70% volume of commercial LPG available in Maharashtra, says State Government
content: The Government says there has been no impact on petrol and diesel supply, adding that the rush at petrol pumps was due to panic buying. But officials quickly wrapped up a press conference when angry questions about disruptions of LPG supply persisted

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Full list of LDF candidates
content: The Kerala Legislative Assembly elections are scheduled to take place on April 9, 2026 and will be held in a single phase to select 140 members of the Assembly

title: What happened in 2021 Kerala Assembly elections: a recap
content: In 2021 Kerala Legislative Assembly elections, the incumbent Left Democratic Front (LDF), led by Communist Party of India (Marxist), secured a historic victory by winning 99 out of 140 seats, retaining power

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Who is in power in Kerala? Key alliances & Opposition leaders
content: Kerala’s political landscape appears to shift slightly following the recent local body elections, where the ruling Left Democratic Front (LDF) faced unexpected setbacks

title: Pinarayi slams Rahul for drawing equivalence between LDF and Hindutva forces in Chhattisgarh
content: Kerala Chief Minister says the Congress leader’s “brazenly misleading” statement smacks of an “impudence stemming from social and political illiteracy and ignorance”; he says the Congress’ “complacency and ideological ambiguity” in politically resisting Hindu majoritarian forces, including their trespasses on minorities, paved the way for the ascendancy of the Sangh Parivar-backed BJP in India

title: Assembly Election 2026: What is the 48-hour ‘silent period’ ahead of voting?
content: Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry are going to the ballot on April 9, 2026. Tamil Nadu is going on April 23. While West Bengal is holding polls in two phases to be held on April 23 and April 29, 2026.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Personalities and political loyalties to define poll outcome in central Kerala
content: The Left Democratic Front had a clear upper hand in the region comprising Ernakulam, Idukki, Kottayam and Alappuzha in 2021 as it bagged 25 of the 37 Assembly seats. But this time, the shifting of political loyalty of some candidates and the fielding of some prominent individuals in some key constituencies have made the contests worth watching.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: From rallies to reels: high-stakes digital face-off for polls
content: Podcasts seem to be the most sought out tool for campaigning this time. From bigwigs like Pinarayi Vijayan to V.D. Satheesan, many candidates have given podcast interviews ahead of the polls, most of which have earned lakhs of views

title: Kerala elections: Supplyco offers 1 kg of ‘puttu’ powder for ₹1 to incentivise voter turnout, but there’s a catch
content: Voters will be eligible to purchase 1 kg of Kerala State Civil Supplies Corporation brand’s ‘puttu’ powder for ₹1 on the two days following the polls, but offer applicable to customers who make purchases worth more than ₹1,000 on non-subsidised items at Supplyco outlets. Voters must present proof by showing indelible ink mark on finger

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: K.K. Rema eyes a second term in Vadakara as LDF confident of a comeback
content: The incumbent MLA is contesting with UDF support again and her LDF rival is veteran socialist leader M.K. Bhaskaran. The NDA nominee is K. Dileep of the BJP

title: CCTVs in police stations: All issues will be sorted out within two weeks, Centre tells Supreme Court
content: Attorney General’s assurance comes as the Supreme Court emphasises the urgent need for accountability and transparency in law enforcement

title: Telangana Food Safety Department demonstrates methods to identify adulterated food
content: People were shown how visual inspection can aid in detecting use of synthetic dyes to adulterate some food items including dried kiwi, fennel seeds, sweet potato, green peas and others

title: Shorebird from Australia spotted over 7,400 km away on island in Andamans: Expert
content: “The sighting marks the first recorded re-sighting of a tagged bird on Narcondam,” said the former assistant director of the Bombay Natural History Society

title: Mumbai’s new age commute: What is Pod Taxi system
content: With an estimated budget of approximately ₹1017 crore, this project is set to transform daily commuting for the estimated four to six lakh commuters traveling to BKC.

title: Urgent cases to be mentioned only before CJI-led Bench: Supreme Court
content: Usually, urgent matters are mentioned for listing and hearing before the senior-most judge of the apex court if the CJI is either unavailable or busy heading a Constitution Bench

title: Punjab and Haryana High Court bars judicial officers from using AI tools to write judgments
content: A letter to all district and sessions judges in Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh stated that the Chief Justice has asked them to direct judicial officers not to use AI tools

title: Nine policemen get death sentence in Sattankulam father-son custodial death case
content: The custodial torture was meted out to the father and son in June 2020, after they were detained at the Sattankulam police station on the mere charge of violating COVID-19 lockdown norms

title: Expelled Punjab Congress leader Navjot Kaur Sidhu launches new political outfit
content: On February 6, Congress general secretary in charge of Punjab, Bhupesh Baghel, announced that Ms. Kaur had been expelled from the party

title: Kerala polls: K.C. Venugopal dares State govt to probe Wayanad funds allegations against Congress
content: Venugopal says the houses the Congress has promised to families in landslide-hit area will be completed ‘within six months of UDF coming to power’; accuses Pinarayi Vijayan of ‘turning the communist party into a corporate entity’

title: Two British nationals jailed for illegal entry into India from Nepal
content: The two hold Nepal visas but were found to be without valid documents for entry into India and were subsequently arrested in November 2025

title: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri to visit Washington DC from April 8 to review ‘full spectrum’ of ties
content: India and U.S. are likely to deliberate on moving forward on the proposed bilateral trade deal during Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri’s trip

title: CM Naidu to launch fire service fleet in Amaravati
content: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu will launch newly procured fire service vehicles and equipment in Amaravati on Wednesday.

title: Assam Assembly elections 2026: Bid to woo new voters in CM Himanta’s long-time constituency Jalukbari
content: With Himanta Biswa Sarma criss-crossing the State as campaigner-in-chief, the challenge for local BJP canvassers in Jalukbari is to ensure healthy numbers in order to match or better the CM’s six-figure margin from 2021

title: 518 lakes out of 697 in J&K either disappeared or receding: CAG
content: The disappeared lakes include 80 lakes falling under the jurisdiction of the Forest Department and 235 Lakes falling under the jurisdiction of Revenue and Agriculture Department

title: Telangana expands emergency and chronic healthcare beyond Hyderabad; cancer care, dialysis services in districts
content: The interventions are aimed at strengthening access, reducing travel burden, and building a structured public health system, as per a senior Health official

title: 2 children killed in bomb attack in Manipur
content: The attack happened in Bishnupur’s Moirang Tronglaobi, which has witnessed consistent gun firing at the height of ethnic conflict in 2023 and 2024

title: Pinarayi Vijayan’s political career timeline
content: As Kerala gears up for the crucial 2026 Assembly elections, Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan finds himself at a pivotal crossroads in his political career

title: Delhi court sends seven foreign nationals accused in terror case to 30 days judicial custody
content: According to the NIA FIR, the accused entered India on tourist visas and travelled to the Northeast without securing mandatory permits

title: 'Already fled to Hyderabad': Himanta mocks Khera as police search his Delhi residence
content: Assam Police searched Congress leader Pawan Khera's Delhi residence following an FIR by Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife over passport allegations. Khera had claimed Sarma's wife held three passports, a charge denied by the BJP. The Chief Minister vowed strong action, accusing Congress of using unauthorized documents and alleging Pakistani links.

title: 12m barrels of crude! India ramps up Venezuela oil imports to 6-yr high amid US-Iran war
content: India, which relies on imports for about 90% of its crude oil needs, has been exploring alternative sources after the conflict affected flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a route that typically handles nearly 40% of its oil supplies.

title: 'The kala in his hand': How Mukesh Kumar became Delhi Capitals' go-to bowler
content: Mukesh Kumar has emerged as an unlikely yet vital weapon for Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026, silencing doubters with discipline and control. Backed by the team management, the Bengal pacer has relied on hard lengths and consistency to deliver.

title: Fired Oracle employee turns Uber driver, says ‘no panic’ thanks to savings
content: Following Oracle's significant layoffs, a former engineer from Bangalore has garnered praise for his calm transition. He returned to his hometown, Bhubaneswar, and began driving for Uber, supported by substantial savings from fixed deposits. This approach, free from debt and modern financial trends, allows him to plan a new business venture with his parents.

title: India’s nuclear push gets boost as breeder reactor reaches criticality; why it matters
content: India's indigenously developed Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam has achieved criticality, a significant step towards nuclear fuel self-reliance. This milestone advances the country's second stage of its nuclear program, aiming to generate more fuel than it consumes and bolster long-term energy security.

title: Watch: Arjun Tendulkar’s pinpoint yorkers light up LSG nets
content: Arjun Tendulkar is impressing Lucknow Super Giants' net sessions with his accurate yorkers, troubling top batters like Rishabh Pant. Videos of his disciplined bowling have gone viral, fueling fan calls for his inclusion. After two seasons with Mumbai Indians, the young pacer is making a strong case for selection at LSG with his consistent performance, despite limited match opportunities so far.

title: Canada has changed Immigration rules; changing Visa fee, work permit rules and more
content: Canada's immigration landscape is shifting from April 2026 with passport and citizenship fee increases, offset by a 30-day processing guarantee. Super visa rules are relaxed, allowing sponsors more flexibility. Provinces gain increased autonomy in immigration assessments, while rural employers benefit from expanded foreign worker quotas.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 20: Ranveer Singh film surges past Rs. 1,025 crore
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has scripted history as the first Hindi film to breach the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone. Globally, the thriller has amassed Rs. 1,622.72 crore, outperforming blockbusters like Jawan. Despite the IPL 2026 season and a 229-minute runtime, the film remains a powerhouse. To boost family viewership, makers have released updated prints with muted abuses and technical fixes. Now the third-highest-grossing Indian film globally, the spy saga eyes the Rs. 1,700 crore mark.

title: SIR row: Mamata claims names of people belonging to 'specific communities' deleted from voter rolls
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has accused authorities of selectively removing names from voter rolls ahead of assembly elections. She stated that nearly 91 lakh voters were deleted post-revision, with her intervention in the Supreme Court restoring about 32 lakh names. Banerjee alleged that specific communities were targeted in these deletions.

title: Oracle gets new CFO, less than a week after 10,000-plus layoffs
content: Oracle has initiated a new week by appointing Hilary Maxson as its new Chief Financial Officer, following recent widespread layoffs. This move comes as the software giant invests heavily in AI infrastructure, taking on significant debt. Maxson, with extensive financial experience, aims to ensure disciplined investment for customer and shareholder value amidst Oracle's ambitious cloud and AI expansion plans.

title: ‘Why engage in such fights?’: Sehwag’s blunt take on Ajinkya Rahane’s outburst
content: Former India opener Virender Sehwag has urged Ajinkya Rahane to respond to criticism with composure. Rahane recently reacted strongly to questions about his strike rate and Cameron Green's bowling. Sehwag believes players should remain neutral to praise and criticism. He cited legends like Amitabh Bachchan and Sachin Tendulkar as examples of handling scrutiny without public reactions.

title: 'To the winner belong the spoils': Trump eyes Iran's oil, says 'I am a businessman first'
content: President Trump suggested the US could seize Iran's oil, framing it as a business opportunity and citing Venezuela as an example. He also indicated a shift in US policy by referencing "to the victor belong the spoils.

title: Kittle linked to Cowboys move as 49ers eye $5M defensive upgrade
content: Jadeveon Clowney is a highly rated edge defender. He last played for the Dallas Cowboys. He is a potential target for the San Francisco 49ers. Their roster has injured players and the farewell of Bryce Huff. Clowney’s beyond impressive 2025 stats and PFF grades could bolster the Niners’ weakest in the league pass rush. However, he told KPRC 2 that he wants a reunion with Lamar Jackson in Baltimore.

title: Mahomes gets boost as KC Current star returns amid injury recovery
content: Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes received a timely boost as KC Current star Temwa Chawinga returned from injury. The reigning MVP’s comeback comes during a tough stretch, offering hope to end a losing streak. Her presence could quickly restore form and energy ahead of a crucial matchup against Gotham FC.

title: Rory McIlroy marriage drama deepens as book reveals divorce twist
content: Rory McIlroy’s short-lived divorce from Erica Stoll in 2024 drew huge attention, especially with Amanda Balionis rumors. A new book by Alan Shipnuck shares inside details, but confirms no affair. The couple reunited within a month, calling it a “wake-up call.” Despite personal challenges, McIlroy continues to focus on golf with family support.

title: Chennai company gifts 12 Audi cars to employees! Check details
content: The visuals from the event show a fleet of identical Audi A4 sedans lined up, suggesting that all recipients were given the same model. The use of identical vehicles indicates a structured reward programme, and the move has prompted discussions around the use of high-value incentives in employee recognition.

title: 5 common shaving mistakes damaging your skin: Real truth about hair removal and skincare
content: Shaving myths debunked by experts reveal that hair doesn't grow back thicker or darker. Using shaving cream is essential for a smooth shave and dull blades cause irritation. Hair growth is hormonal, not affected by shaving frequency. Ingrown hairs are preventable with proper technique. Shaving can be safe and effective for smooth, healthy skin.

title: IPL 2026: After KKR-PBKS washout, will rain hit RR vs MI in Guwahati too?
content: Rain looms over the Rajasthan Royals vs Mumbai Indians IPL clash in Guwahati. While evening conditions are expected to improve, a significant afternoon shower threat could lead to delays. A minimum five-overs-a-side match requires play to start by 10:56 pm. A washout would see both teams share points, a scenario no side desires early in the tournament.

title: Army unveils roadmap for unmanned aerial systems, loitering munitions
content: The Indian Army has unveiled a technology roadmap for drones and loitering munitions, aiming to bolster domestic capabilities. This initiative seeks to integrate industry, R&D, and academia to meet diverse operational needs, from high-altitude reconnaissance to coastal strike support and logistics. The plan prioritizes countering enemy drones and enhancing indigenous defense manufacturing, encouraging MSME and startup participation.

title: 'UDF will come to power': Shashi Tharoor cites strong 'anti-incumbency' mood ahead of Kerala polls
content: Speaking about his campaign experiences across the state, Tharoor said, "I participated in campaigns across 59 constituencies in Keralam. The UDF will come to power in Keralam. There is a feeling of anti-incumbency among the people. I could understand this directly through my campaign visits across constituencies."

title: India in talks with Trump admin for Chabahar port sanctions waiver; why it’s important
content: New Delhi is in talks with the Donald Trump administration to secure an extension of the sanctions waiver for the port, which is set to expire at the end of this month.

title: Air India revises fuel surcharge amid energy crunch; here’s how much more you will pay
content: Air India group has revised its fuel surcharge for domestic and international flights due to rising oil prices and Middle East tensions. Domestic fares will now be distance-linked, while international surcharges see steeper increases, reflecting global jet fuel price surges and increased refinery margins. These changes aim to partially offset rising operational costs for the airline.

title: Bengaluru: Labourer 'develops feelings' for friend's wife, slits his throat after drinking spree
content: A construction labourer was murdered by his friend after a night of drinking near a bar in Bengaluru. The accused allegedly killed the victim to pursue a relationship with the deceased's wife, who had confided in him about domestic abuse. The body was found stuffed in a gunny bag.

title: Steve Smith left fuming as Farhan denies single, fans recall Babar Azam drama
content: A tense moment in the PSL saw Steve Smith denied a single by Sahibzada Farhan, sparking comparisons to Smith's own controversial refusal of a run to Babar Azam in the BBL. While fans cried 'revenge,' Farhan's move seemed tactical, unlike Smith's earlier strategic play. Smith still anchored Multan's chase to victory.

title: God knows how many parts Pak will be divided this time’: Rajnath hits out at Khawaja Asif
content: India's Defence Minister Rajnath Singh has strongly criticized Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif for his provocative statement targeting Kolkata. Singh warned Islamabad against repeating past mistakes, referencing the 1971 division of Pakistan. This exchange follows Asif's threat to strike Kolkata if India launches a "false-flag operation."

title: 'List stands frozen': Nearly 91 lakh voters deleted from Bengal rolls after SIR
content: Nearly 91 lakh names were removed from West Bengal's electoral rolls after a special revision exercise. The Election Commission's data shows significant deletions, impacting the voter base. While some deletions were finalized, others remain under adjudication. Electoral rolls for the first phase of assembly elections have now been frozen.

title: After son’s body went unclaimed, this man took a step which has honoured 25k dead so far
content: A bicycle mechanic from Ayodhya, Mohammad Sharif, transformed profound personal grief into a lifelong mission. After losing his son, he vowed to provide dignified final rites for unclaimed bodies, regardless of faith. For decades, he personally arranged cremations and burials, earning him the affectionate title 'Sharif Chacha' and culminating in a prestigious Padma Shri award for his selfless service.

title: 17 India-bound vessels with LPG, LNG, crude await transit near Hormuz
content: Seventeen India-bound ships carrying energy products, including LPG, LNG, and crude oil, are waiting west of the Strait of Hormuz. Eight Indian vessels and one foreign-flagged ship have already transited the conflict-hit strait. The shipping ministry is coordinating with the external affairs ministry to ensure the safe passage of these vessels.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's biggest challenge yet! Trial by fire vs Jasprit Bumrah
content: Teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faces his ultimate T20 test against Jasprit Bumrah. After a sensational IPL debut, the 15-year-old, who skipped board exams for cricket, has impressed with explosive knocks. This clash against the world's best bowler could define his burgeoning career, with teams analyzing his game. Mumbai Indians remain unfazed, preparing as usual.

title: Bengal woman brutally attacked at Bengaluru house over 'flesh trade’ claim; 5 held
content: Five individuals, including three women, have been apprehended in Varthur for allegedly breaking into a woman's home and assaulting her. The group, daily wage workers from West Bengal, accused the victim of running a flesh trade racket.

title: Nitesh Tiwari teases Yash's Ravana will not be a traditional villain in Ramayana
content: Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana' franchise, starring Ranbir Kapoor and Yash, promises a complex portrayal of Ravana. Director Tiwari revealed Yash's character will be explored beyond a simple villain, highlighting his warrior, musician, scholar, and devotee facets. This depth, he explained, serves a crucial lesson on the destructive power of vengeance and ego, even amidst great qualities.

title: IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals' Mitchell Starc likely to miss at least three more games
content: Australian pacer Mitchell Starc is set to miss at least three more IPL games for Delhi Capitals due to shoulder and elbow injuries. He's unlikely to join the squad before April 20. Despite his absence, the Axar Patel-led Delhi side has secured two wins. Starc, however, remains committed to joining the team as soon as he recovers.

title: ‘Will trek again’: Kerala techie lost in forest for 4 days recalls ‘beautiful nights’, not fear
content: G S Sharanya, an IT professional from Kerala, was lost for four days in a dense forest near Tadiandamol. She survived by drinking stream water and remained safe. Sharanya was eventually found by locals. The ordeal has not diminished her love for trekking and the wilderness. She plans future trips.

title: Mini Cooper Crashes Into Bike In Goa, Woman Working At 5-Star Hotel Dies
content: Darius Dias, who was driving the Mini Cooper, is the son of Andrew Dias, a businessman with a bakery and hotels in Goa

title: Delhi Man Calls 8-Year-Old Girl Home To Fix Speaker, Sexually Assaults Her
content: The accused, identified as Sadaruddin, aged 32, and a resident of Aman Vihar, Delhi, was arrested. Investigation revealed that he is known to the victim's father, and both belong to the same community.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Key Saudi-Bahrain Bridge Closed Amid Iran Threats, Trump's Hormuz Deadline Nears
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: Trump's vow to attack Iran if the Strait of Hormuz is not opened by Tuesday has prompted Tehran to call him "delusional".

title: Assam Cops At Pawan Khera's Home Amid Himanta Sarma's Wife's Passport Row
content: Pawan Khera, the chief of Congress' Media and Publicity Department, had in a press conference on Sunday alleged that Himanta Sarma's wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, owns three passports and that the family has business interests worth Rs 52,000 crore in

title: War-Fuelled Divorce: How Right-Wing Podcasters Flipped Stance Against Trump
content: Joe Rogan is joined by several others from his field, who have soured on Trump, when they were vocal supporters during his 2024 Presidential campaign.

title: Kerala: The Existential Election For The Left

title: Masters 2026: Ranking all 91 players in the field
content: for the first time in his career, Rory McIlroy will drive down Magnolia Lane this week as a Masters champion . a year ago, he made a 4-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole to defeat england's Justin Rose on the first hole of a sudden-death playoff . with his long-awaited Masters victory, Mcilroy became only the sixth golfer to complete the career Grand Slam .

title: Arsenal need Mikel Arteta to step up now to avoid disaster again
content: Mikel Arteta's side are just five victories from success in the two competitions it has become so desperate to win: the Premier League and Champions League . the gunners lost 2-1 to Southampton in the FA Cup quarterfinals on saturday . artet awoke on Sunday with the UEFA Champions League quarterfinal first leg on the horizon .
